skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: A bakery wants to estimate the percent of pastries produced in a typical week that are damaged in packaging. A random sample of 420 pastries produced in a certain week was inspected. Based on the sample, it is estimated that 18% of all pastries produced by the bakery this week were damaged, with an associated margin of error of 2.7%. Based on the estimate and associated margin of error, which of the following is most appropriate?

answerOptions.content: It is plausible that more than 20.7% of the pastries are damaged.
It is plausible that between 15.3% and 20.7% of the pastries are damaged.

rationale:
Choice D is correct. The plausible range is 18% ± 2.7%, i.e., 15.3% to 20.7%.

Choices A and B treat point values as exact. Choice C is outside the plausible interval.
